text: 13.9g of fat. omega 3 is exactly the kind of fat support you want from a meat. for meats, fat quality matters a lot once the protein is already there.
0g of carbs. outside of that, the profile is pretty quiet. in this category, carbs barely matter.
18.6g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. strong protein is expected here, so the rest of the profile decides how high it climbs.
vitamin b12 at 300% daily value, vitamin d at 90% daily value stand out most. that is major vitamin support.

on the plus side: high satiety protein source because that is a major real-world strength for meat. complete protein delivery is reliable because the category is supposed to do this well. easy to centre a meal around because practical meal value matters.

on the weaker side: fat quality can go sideways fast because some meats are strong until the fat profile shows up. cost and storage can be a pain because practical downsides still count. brings no fibre support because that leaves a nutrition gap the rest of the meal has to cover.
S tier. Best when you want efficient protein and can accept the category tradeoffs that come with it.
